prompts.text: Pixel art of a suburban neighborhood at dawn where every single mailbox has been replaced with a CRT monitor displaying "YOU'VE GOT DEALS." One neighbor is confused. Another is already loading their car. A lobster sits on the fire hydrant watching.

Pixel art of a Blockbuster Video return slot but someone is shoving a vintage lamp through it. The employee inside looks horrified. A sign reads "LATE FEE: $3.50." The store computer screen shows flip-ly.net in the background.

Pixel art of a 90s kid's bedroom with a Gateway 2000 cow-box computer. The kid is asleep at the keyboard. The monitor shows a garage sale search loading at 56k. A stuffed lobster plushie sits on the bed. Glow-in-the-dark stars on the ceiling.

Pixel art of a medieval blacksmith's forge but instead of swords, he's hammering a "GARAGE SALE SATURDAY 7AM" sign out of molten metal. Sparks fly. A lobster watches from the anvil. The forge fire is green like a terminal screen.

Pixel art of an astronaut on the moon planting a flag that says "ESTATE SALE — EVERYTHING MUST GO." Earth is visible in the background. The lunar rover has a bumper sticker: "I'd rather be flipping." A tiny lobster floats in zero gravity.

Pixel art of a 1990s school computer lab. Every monitor shows a different Craigslist garage sale listing. The teacher is asleep. One kid in the back has found the Classified Access page. Green text reflects on his glasses.

Pixel art of a fortune teller's tent at a carnival. The crystal ball shows a glowing image of a vintage KitchenAid mixer with "DEAL SCORE: 10/10" floating above it. The fortune teller is a robot butler. The customer's jaw is dropped.

Pixel art of a drive-in movie theater at night. The massive screen is showing flip-ly.net instead of a movie. Cars are honking. Someone in a convertible is taking notes. The concession stand sign reads "POPCORN $2 / LAMPS $3."

Pixel art of Santa's workshop but the elves are wrapping garage sale finds instead of toys. One elf holds up a ceramic frog with pride. Santa's list on the wall is actually a Craigslist printout. A lobster ornament hangs on the tree.

Pixel art of a spy movie scene — a briefcase handoff in a dark alley. Inside the briefcase: a pristine Beanie Baby still in the case. Both men wear sunglasses at night. A neon sign behind them flickers "FLIP-LY.NET." Rain falls in pixel drops.

Pixel art of a 90s hacker in a dark room surrounded by six monitors. Five show garage sale listings scrolling in green text. The sixth shows "ACCESS DENIED — REFERRED TO LOBSTER COUNCIL." Empty Mountain Dew cans everywhere. Red and blue LED glow.

Pixel art of a suburban garage sale from the buyer's POV looking at a table of items. Price tags visible. A vintage lamp ($3), a box of records ($5), a cursed painting ($1). The seller in the background is a robot butler in an apron.

Pixel art of a news anchor desk. Breaking news banner reads "LOCAL MAN FINDS $400 TABLE FOR $20 — NEIGHBORS SUSPICIOUS." The anchor is a lobster in a suit. The weather map behind shows garage sale hotspots instead of temperatures.

Pixel art of a Pokemon battle screen but instead of Pokemon, it's "WILD VINTAGE LAMP appeared!" The trainer menu shows: FIGHT / BAG / FLIP / RUN. The lamp has HP bar showing "DEAL SCORE: 8/10." The trainer is wearing a lobster hat.

Pixel art of a library with impossibly tall shelves but instead of books, every shelf holds VHS tapes, lava lamps, and Precious Moments figurines. A librarian robot butler whispers "the good stuff is in the back." Dust motes float in light beams.

Pixel art of a 90s chat room on a CRT screen. Usernames visible: xX_TableFlipper_Xx, LampQueen_Dallas, COUCH_DESTROYER_99. They're arguing about whether a $5 chair is worth flipping. One user types "check the source." Green text on black.

Pixel art of a pirate ship but the treasure chest is full of garage sale finds — a waffle iron, vintage Pyrex, old Nintendo cartridges. The pirate captain holds up a lamp triumphantly. The Jolly Roger flag has a lobster skull. Sunset ocean behind.

Pixel art of a Windows 98 Blue Screen of Death but the error message reads "FATAL ERROR: TOO MANY DEALS DETECTED IN YOUR AREA. THE BUTLER HAS CRASHED. LOBSTER OVERFLOW IN SECTOR 7. Press any key to flip." White text on blue.

Pixel art of a bowling alley where all the pins are vintage lamps. The bowling ball is a lobster curled into a ball. The scoreboard shows player names from the BBS testimonials. The carpet is that classic 90s neon pattern.

Pixel art of a Jurassic Park gate but instead of "JURASSIC PARK" the sign reads "GARAGE PARK — WHERE DEALS COME ALIVE." A velociraptor carries a vintage chair in its mouth. Jeeps are loaded with estate sale finds. A lobster sits on the gate.

Pixel art of an old vending machine in a dimly lit hallway. Instead of snacks, every slot contains a garage sale item with a price tag. Slot A7 has a lamp for $3. The digital display reads "INSERT DEAL CODE." A lobster sticker is on the glass.

Pixel art of a courtroom. The judge is a lobster with a tiny gavel. The defendant is a Comic Sans font on trial. The evidence board shows screenshots of flip-ly.net. The jury is 12 Clippy paperclips. The bailiff is a robot butler.

Pixel art of a 90s infomercial scene. "BUT WAIT, THERE'S MORE!" text at the bottom. A person demonstrating a lamp they found for $3. Before/after split screen showing sad person without lamp and ecstatic person with lamp. Call now number: 1-800-LOBSTER.

Pixel art of a heist movie planning scene. Five characters around a table with blueprints of a suburban house. The blueprints show the garage with X marks on valuable items. The plan reads "ARRIVE 6:58 AM. TARGET: KITCHENAID." The getaway car is a minivan.

Pixel art of a space mission control room during a launch. All screens show garage sale data instead of telemetry. Flight director shouts commands. Main screen reads "T-MINUS 6 HOURS TO THURSDAY DIGEST." A lobster is mission commander.

Pixel art of a medieval scroll being unfurled on a stone table. The scroll contains Craigslist listings in old English font. A monk with a quill is transcribing deals. Candlelight flickers. A lobster illumination decorates the margin.

Pixel art of a 90s mall food court. Every restaurant sign is replaced: "McFlips," "Burger Deals," "Taco Sale." Teens sit at tables browsing flip-ly on their Sidekicks. The fountain in the center has a golden lobster statue.

Pixel art of a weathered wanted poster nailed to a wooden post in a western town. "WANTED: THE BUTLER" with a pixelated robot butler face. "REWARD: ONE VINTAGE LAMP." "LAST SEEN: FLIP-LY.NET." Tumbleweed rolls past. Sunset colors.

Pixel art of a grocery store checkout but every item on the conveyor belt is a garage sale find. The cashier scans a ceramic cat — price shows $0.50. The customer behind is impatient. The tabloid magazine rack shows "LOBSTER COUNCIL EXPOSED" headline.

Pixel art of a traffic jam on a suburban street at 6:55 AM Saturday morning. Every car is heading to the same garage sale. Drivers honking. A helicopter overhead with a news banner "ESTATE SALE GRIDLOCK." One car has a lobster bumper sticker.

Pixel art of an elevator with a digital floor display. The floors read: B2-LAMPS, B1-RECORDS, G-FURNITURE, 1-ELECTRONICS, 2-VINTAGE, 3-CLASSIFIED ACCESS. A robot butler and a confused person stand inside. Elevator music notes float.

Pixel art of a dentist's waiting room but every magazine on the table is a printed flip-ly.net weekly digest. The TV in the corner shows garage sale highlights. The fish tank has a tiny lobster. The receptionist window says "NEXT DEAL: NOW."

Pixel art of a child's lemonade stand but they're selling garage sale items instead. Sign reads "FLIPPED GOODS — CHEAP." A grown adult is examining a lamp seriously. The kid has a calculator out. A lobster plushie is the mascot.

Pixel art of a museum exhibit behind velvet ropes. A single lamp on a white pedestal under a spotlight. The placard reads "VINTAGE TIFFANY-STYLE LAMP — ACQUIRED: GARAGE SALE, $3 — ESTIMATED VALUE: $450." Museum visitors gasp. A lobster is the security guard.

Pixel art of a fast food drive-through menu board but every item is a garage sale category. "COMBO 1: Furniture + Electronics — $12." "HAPPY MEAL: Random box of stuff — $2." The drive-through speaker has a lobster claw holding the microphone.

Pixel art of a 90s Geocities webpage being built in Microsoft FrontPage. The screen shows Comic Sans text, under construction GIFs, a hit counter, and a flaming text banner that reads "WELCOME TO MY GARAGE SALE PAGE." The developer drinks Surge soda.

Pixel art of a submarine control room. The periscope view shows a suburban neighborhood from above with garage sales marked in red targeting circles. The captain shouts "DEALS DETECTED — BEARING 270." Sonar pings show lamp-shaped blips.

Pixel art of a gym but everyone is training for garage sales. One person practices sprinting to a driveway. Another does bicep curls with a box of records. The motivational poster reads "CHAMPIONS ARRIVE AT 6:58 AM." A lobster is the personal trainer.

Pixel art of a horror movie poster. Title: "THE LAMP." A suburban house at night, single window glowing with an eerie lamp light. Tagline: "They said it was $3. They were wrong." Rating: "DEAL SCORE: CURSED/10." A lobster shadow on the lawn.

Pixel art of an ice cream truck but it's covered in garage sale ads. The jingle music notes floating out are in Comic Sans font. Kids are running toward it with dollar bills. The ice cream man is a robot butler serving vintage Pyrex instead of popsicles.

Pixel art of a detective interrogation room. A lamp is sitting in the chair being questioned. The detective slams the table: "WHERE WERE YOU MANUFACTURED?" The one-way mirror shows other lamps watching nervously. Case file on table reads "FLIP-LY CASE #420."

Pixel art of a 90s AOL Instant Messenger window. Buddy list showing: ButlerBot69 (online), LobsterKing (away), xX_DealSniper_Xx (online). Chat window open with someone asking "did u find the password yet?" Reply: "check below the counter." Away message: "gone flipping brb."

Pixel art of a wedding ceremony but the altar is a garage sale folding table. The bride and groom exchange vintage rings. The officiant is a robot butler. The flower girl throws Craigslist confetti. The wedding arch is made of stacked vintage suitcases. A lobster is the ring bearer.

Pixel art of a 90s screensaver — the classic flying toasters — but the toasters are replaced with flying lamps, flying lobsters, and flying "DEAL SCORE 10/10" badges. They bounce off the edges of a CRT monitor. One toaster remains, confused among the chaos. Starfield background.

Pixel art of a bank vault door slowly opening to reveal inside: not gold bars, but perfectly organized shelves of the best garage sale finds ever. Vintage guitars, mint-condition vinyl, pristine mid-century furniture. A single spotlight. A lobster security camera watches from above. The vault code panel shows "SECTOR 7."

Pixel art of a Mount Rushmore-style cliff carving but the four faces are: the robot butler, Clippy, the AOL running man, and a lobster. Tourists take photos below. The gift shop sells "I FOUND THE EASTER EGG" t-shirts. An American flag flies above. Majestic pixel clouds.

Pixel art of a 90s airport baggage carousel but instead of suitcases, vintage lamps, record players, and ceramic animals rotate past. Travelers grab items frantically. One guy missed his lamp and watches it go around again. The flight board reads "ARRIVING: DEALS FROM DALLAS." A lobster TSA agent waves someone through.

Pixel art of a Bob Ross painting scene. Lobster Bob Ross stands at an easel painting "happy little garage sales." The canvas shows a perfect suburban street with sale signs. His palette has colors named "Lamp Gold," "Ceramic Cat Beige," and "Craigslist Green." A tiny squirrel watches from the easel. Afro is immaculate.

Pixel art of a submarine sandwich shop but the menu board lists garage sale categories. "THE FLIPPER: Vintage Electronics, Extra Vinyl, Hold the Junk — $6.99." The sandwich artist is a robot butler. The customer points at the menu confused. Checkered floor tiles. A lobster eats a footlong in the corner booth.

Pixel art of two astronauts floating in a space station looking out the window at Earth. One points down at a tiny glowing suburb. "Is that a garage sale?" The other checks a tablet showing flip-ly.net. "DEAL SCORE: 9/10 — TOO FAR." Mission patches on their suits read "OPERATION: FLIP." A lobster floats between them.

Pixel art of a 90s Blockbuster on its last day. "EVERYTHING MUST GO" banner. Customers fighting over DVDs. A kid in the back discovers a hidden VHS labeled "THE LOBSTER TAPES — DO NOT RENT." The clerk doesn't care. The drop box outside is already full. Sunset through the windows.

Pixel art of a lobster DJ at a massive outdoor rave. The DJ booth is a repurposed garage sale folding table. Giant speakers made of stacked CRT monitors. The crowd is silhouettes with glow sticks. The vinyl record spinning says "FLIP SIDE B." Laser beams spell out "DEALS" in the sky.

Pixel art of a dog show competition ring but every dog is carrying a garage sale item in its mouth. Golden retriever with a lamp. Poodle with a vintage handbag. Chihuahua dragging a full-size painting. The judge is a lobster with a clipboard. Trophy reads "BEST IN FLIP." Crowd cheers.

Pixel art of a 90s kid standing in a Toys R Us aisle but every shelf has garage sale items instead of toys. The kid looks up in awe at a wall of vintage board games. Geoffrey the Giraffe has been replaced with a giant lobster mascot. The PA speaker announces "CLEANUP IN THE DEALS AISLE."

Pixel art of a NASCAR pit stop but the crew is loading garage sale furniture into the car instead of changing tires. Driver screams from the window. A recliner is strapped to the roof. The pit crew wears lobster-red jumpsuits. The jumbotron reads "LAP 42 — DEAL SCORE: 11/10." Smoke and speed lines.

Pixel art of a lobster sitting alone in a therapist's office on a tiny couch. The therapist is a ceramic cat taking notes. The lobster says "They keep trying 'admin' as the password." The therapist nods. A box of tissues on the side table. Diploma on the wall reads "PhD in Crustacean Feelings." Warm lamp light.

Pixel art of a cooking show kitchen. The host holds up a vintage lamp and says "TODAY WE'RE FLIPPING THIS." Ingredients on the counter: spray paint, sandpaper, fairy lights, and audacity. The audience gasps. A lobster sous chef takes notes. The chyron reads "FLIP-LY KITCHEN — SEASON 3, EP 12."

Pixel art of a Wild West saloon. A lobster in a cowboy hat kicks open the swinging doors. Everyone freezes. The bartender slowly slides a lamp across the bar. Piano player stops. A wanted poster on the wall shows a ceramic cat. Tumbleweed rolls through the door behind the lobster. Spurs clink.

Pixel art of a crossfit gym but all the equipment is garage sale items. Someone deadlifts a vintage typewriter. Another does box jumps on stacked milk crates. The whiteboard WOD reads "50 LAMP PRESSES / 30 RECORD CURLS / 20 CERAMIC CAT TOSSES." A lobster times them with a stopwatch.

Pixel art of a lobster and a robot butler playing chess in a park. The chess pieces are all miniature garage sale items — lamp bishop, ceramic cat queen, record player rook. An old man on the next bench reads a newspaper with headline "LOCAL GARAGE SALE BREAKS ATTENDANCE RECORD." Autumn leaves fall.

Pixel art of a 90s mall photo studio with the laser background. A family of lobsters poses — dad lobster, mom lobster, three baby lobsters. The photographer is a robot butler behind a camera. The sample photos on the wall all feature lamps. The carpet is peak 90s purple and teal geometry.

Pixel art of a lobster lifeguard on a beach tower blowing a whistle. Below, people aren't drowning — they're fighting over a vintage surfboard someone put out for $5. A seagull carries away a Precious Moments figurine. The lifeguard tower has a flip-ly.net bumper sticker. Beach umbrella reads "DEALS OR DIE."

Pixel art of a Japanese game show set. A contestant stands on a platform over a foam pit. The host (a lobster in a sparkly suit) holds up a lamp and asks "WHAT IS THE DEAL SCORE?" Wrong answer — the platform drops. The audience is all ceramic cats. Confetti cannons ready. Neon Japanese text everywhere.

Pixel art of the Fresh Prince of Bel-Air opening but instead of a basketball court, Will is at a garage sale in West Philadelphia. Carlton does the Carlton dance while holding a vintage lamp. Geoffrey the butler is replaced by a robot butler. The cab driver is a lobster. Graffiti wall reads "FLIP-LY.NET."

Pixel art of the Seinfeld apartment but every surface is covered in garage sale finds. Kramer slides through the door holding a ceramic lobster. Jerry at his desk: "What's the DEAL with deal scores?" George on the couch surrounded by Beanie Babies. The Superman magnet is replaced with a lobster magnet.

Pixel art of a "This Is Fine" meme scene but the dog is a lobster in a top hat, the room is a garage sale that's gone viral, and the flames are actually price tags floating everywhere. The coffee mug reads "DEAL SCORE: 10/10." The newspaper headline: "EVERYTHING MUST GO."

Pixel art of the Rugrats babies crawling through a giant garage sale. Tommy has a screwdriver and a vintage lamp. Chuckie is scared of a ceramic cat. Angelica demands the best finds. Reptar has been replaced with a giant lobster toy. The playpen is made of stacked milk crates.

Pixel art of the X-Files office. Mulder's "I WANT TO BELIEVE" poster shows a vintage lamp floating in a beam of light. Scully examines a ceramic frog with a magnifying glass. The filing cabinet is labeled "UNSOLVED DEALS." A lobster watches from the air vent. Truth is out there at flip-ly.net.

Pixel art of a "Distracted Boyfriend" meme. The boyfriend looks at a "$3 VINTAGE LAMP" while his girlfriend "RESPONSIBLE SPENDING" glares. The other woman is the lamp on a garage sale table. Everyone is pixelated. A lobster photographs the whole scene from behind a bush.

Pixel art of Steve Urkel crashing through a wall into a garage sale. "Did I flip that?" Broken ceramics everywhere. Laura Winslow facepalms. Carl holds a ruined lamp. Stefan Urquelle emerges from behind a rack of vintage suits. A lobster sits in the transformation chamber.

Pixel art of the "Woman Yelling at Cat" meme but the woman is yelling "THAT LAMP IS WORTH $400!" and the confused cat at the table is surrounded by garage sale price tags. The table has a plate of vintage Pyrex instead of salad. A lobster referee stands between them.

Pixel art of Saved by the Bell's The Max diner. Zack Morris calls a time-out (freeze frame) mid-garage-sale-negotiation. Screech's robot Kevin has been upgraded to a deal-finding bot. Slater arm-wrestles for a vintage boombox. Lisa shops vintage fashion. A lobster is the new Max.

Pixel art of the "Expanding Brain" meme in four panels. Panel 1 (small brain): "Buying new at retail." Panel 2: "Checking Craigslist." Panel 3: "Waking up at 6 AM for garage sales." Panel 4 (galaxy brain): "Using flip-ly.net to find everything." A lobster ascends in the final panel.

Pixel art of the Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles in the sewer lair but instead of pizza boxes, it's full of garage sale finds. Leonardo examines a vintage katana display ($2). Donatello tinkers with a broken VCR. Michelangelo: "COWABUNGA DEALS, DUDE!" Splinter meditates on a beanbag. A lobster watches from the shadows.

Pixel art of the "Drake Approving/Disapproving" meme. Top panel (disapproving): Drake at a retail store with full-price tags. Bottom panel (approving): Drake at a garage sale finding a vintage jacket for $5. Both panels in pixel art style. A lobster gives a thumbs up in the corner.

Pixel art of the Friends couch from Central Perk but it's at a garage sale with a $15 price tag. All six friends argue about who saw it first. The orange couch has a "DEAL SCORE: ICONIC/10" floating above it. Gunther watches sadly from behind a table of mugs. A lobster barista serves coffee.

Pixel art of Bill Nye the Science Guy's lab but he's demonstrating "THE SCIENCE OF FLIPPING." Beakers contain liquid labeled "PROFIT MARGIN" and "DEAL JUICE." The whiteboard shows a formula: (Garage Sale Price) x (eBay Markup) = Victory. A lobster lab assistant takes notes. Theme song arrows everywhere.

Pixel art of a "Success Kid" meme. The determined toddler clutches a vintage lamp instead of sand. Background text: "ARRIVED AT 6:58 AM — GOT THE LAST KITCHENAID." Pixel fist pump. A lobster high-fives from off-screen. Deal score badge floats overhead.

Pixel art of the Magic School Bus but Ms. Frizzle drives through a neighborhood of garage sales. "SEATBELTS, EVERYONE!" The bus shrinks to fit inside a vintage lamp. Arnold: "I knew I should have stayed home." The Friz wears lobster earrings. The bus license plate reads "FL1P-LY."

Pixel art of Doug Funnie writing in his journal. "Dear Journal, today I found a vintage Beets album at a garage sale for $1. Skeeter said 'honk honk' about the deal. Patti was impressed. Roger tried to outbid me." Porkchop wears a lobster costume. Quailman poster replaced with a flip-ly.net poster.

Pixel art of the "One Does Not Simply" Boromir meme but he's at the gates of a massive estate sale. "ONE DOES NOT SIMPLY WALK PAST A $3 LAMP." Behind him, garage sale chaos. Shoppers storming in like orcs. A lobster Gandalf holds up a staff: "YOU SHALL NOT OVERPAY."

Pixel art of the Power Rangers morphing sequence but instead of dinosaurs, their Zords are garage sale items. Red Ranger pilots a giant lamp. Blue Ranger commands a massive ceramic cat. The Megazord is assembled from vintage furniture. A lobster is Zordon on the view screen. "IT'S FLIPPIN' TIME!"

Pixel art of Pinky and the Brain in their cage at Acme Labs. Brain's plan on the chalkboard: "STEP 1: Acquire all garage sale lamps. STEP 2: Control the vintage lamp market. STEP 3: TAKE OVER THE WORLD." Pinky: "NARF! But what about flip-ly.net, Brain?" A lobster scientist watches through the glass.

Pixel art of the "Stonks" meme man standing in front of a stock ticker that shows LAMP, PYREX, and VINYL all going up. Green arrows everywhere. The man wears a lobster tie. The ticker at the bottom reads "FLIP-LY.NET — DEALS MARKET: BULLISH." Pixelated sunglasses descend.

Pixel art of Legends of the Hidden Temple. The Olmec head asks "WHICH GARAGE SALE HAS THE HIDDEN LAMP?" Teams: Red Lobsters, Blue Butlers, Green Flippers. A kid runs through the temple grabbing vintage items instead of pendants. Temple guards are ceramic cats. Silver monkey replaced with a silver lobster.

Pixel art of TRL on MTV. Carson Daly hosts the countdown: "#1 DEAL OF THE WEEK." Times Square crowd outside holds signs: "WE WANT LAMPS." The video playing on screen shows a garage sale montage. Request phone lines are flooded. A lobster calls in from a payphone.

Pixel art of the "Surprised Pikachu" meme but Pikachu is at a garage sale staring at a $2 vintage Nintendo 64 still in box. Mouth wide open. Lightning bolt cheeks glow. The seller doesn't know what they have. A lobster Trainer throws a Poke Ball labeled "DEAL." Pixel sparkles everywhere.

Pixel art of the "Two Buttons" meme. A sweating man must choose between "SLEEP IN ON SATURDAY" and "6 AM ESTATE SALE — POSSIBLE KITCHENAID." Both buttons glow. Sweat drops are pixelated. A lobster angel and lobster devil sit on each shoulder. The devil button is already being pressed.

Pixel art of Dexter's Laboratory. Dexter builds a "DEAL-FINDING-INATOR" machine. DeeDee keeps pressing buttons and accidentally buying everything on flip-ly.net. "DEEDEE! GET OUT OF MY LABORATORY SALE!" The machine has a lobster-claw grabber arm. Monitor shows "DEAL SCORE: CALCULATING..."

Pixel art of the "This is Sparta" scene from 300 but the Spartan king kicks a bad deal into a pit. "THIS! IS! OVERPRICED!" An army of garage sale shoppers stands behind him in formation. Their shields have lobster emblems. The pit is filled with retail receipts. Dramatic pixel lighting.

Pixel art of the Spice Girls but they're the Flip Girls — Scary Flip, Sporty Flip, Baby Flip, Ginger Flip, Posh Flip. Each holds a different garage sale find. They stand on a Union Jack made of Craigslist printouts. Stage backdrop reads "FLIP-LY WORLD TOUR '98." A lobster is their manager.

Pixel art of Where in the World is Carmen Sandiego but Carmen stole the world's greatest garage sale find. ACME agents investigate. Clue: "She was last seen at a suburban estate sale." The world map has red pins at flip-ly deal locations. A lobster detective adjusts a magnifying glass. "DO IT, ROCKAPELLA!"

Pixel art of the "Awkward Look Monkey Puppet" meme. The monkey is at a garage sale and accidentally broke a ceramic cat. The seller stares. The monkey slowly looks away. Price tag on the broken cat: $0.50. A lobster behind the monkey whispers "just put it back." Side-eye intensifies.

Pixel art of Home Alone's Kevin McCallister defending a garage sale from adult haul-everything buyers. Booby traps everywhere: paint cans swing over lamp tables, marbles on the driveway, a blow torch behind the vintage records. Harry and Marv slip on ice. Kevin grins. A lobster operates the zip line.

Pixel art of Wayne's World basement set but Wayne and Garth are reviewing garage sale finds. "EXCELLENT!" Wayne holds up a vintage guitar. Garth examines a lava lamp. "PARTY ON, FLIPPERS!" The backdrop has Alice Cooper replaced with a giant lobster. Mirthmobile parked outside loaded with finds.

Pixel art of the "Shut Up and Take My Money" Fry meme from Futurama. Fry waves cash at a garage sale table piled with vintage video games. The seller is Bender: "BITE MY SHINY DEAL." Leela facepalms. Professor Farnsworth: "Good news, everyone! I found a $2 lamp!" A lobster Nibbler steals a Beanie Baby.

Pixel art of the Oregon Trail game screen. "You have arrived at a GARAGE SALE." Options: "LOOK AROUND / BUY LAMP / FORD THE RIVER OF VINYL." Your wagon capacity shows: "LAMPS: 7, CERAMIC CATS: 4, HEALTH: DEAL FEVER." A tombstone nearby reads "Here lies Carl — couldn't pass up a $3 lamp." Lobster oxen pull the wagon.

Pixel art of the "Is This a Pigeon?" meme. An anime character holds up a clearly valuable vintage Tiffany lamp. Caption: "IS THIS A $3 GARAGE SALE FIND?" The butterfly is actually labeled "ANTIQUES ROADSHOW MOMENT." A lobster appraiser faints in the background. Flowers bloom pixel by pixel.

Pixel art of Kenan and Kel. Kel crashes through a garage sale table holding orange soda. "AWW HERE IT GOES!" Kenan facepalms beside a ruined display of vintage records. The "Good Burger" sign in the background reads "Good Deals." A lobster wearing a fast food hat watches. "Welcome to Good Deals, home of the Good Deals!"

Pixel art of the "Surprised Tom" meme from Tom and Jerry. Tom's eyes bug out seeing a vintage lamp at a yard sale priced at $1. Jerry is already carrying it away. Tom chases through a maze of garage sale tables. Anvils replaced with ceramic cats. A lobster sits on a fence watching the chaos unfold.

Pixel art of the "Galaxy Brain" evolution but for garage sale shoppers. Stage 1: walks past garage sales. Stage 2: stops occasionally. Stage 3: wakes up early on Saturdays. Stage 4: has a route planned, flip-ly.net loaded, and a lobster co-pilot. Stage 5: transcends time and space, surrounded by vintage lamps.

Pixel art of a "Keep Calm" poster but it reads "KEEP CALM AND CHECK FLIP-LY.NET." The crown is replaced with a lobster. The background is the classic CRT monitor green. Below in small text: "DEAL SCORE: KEEP CALM/10." Pixel Union Jack border. Tea cup has a tiny ceramic cat on the saucer.

Pixel art of Beavis and Butt-Head on their couch but they're watching garage sale haul videos instead of music videos. Beavis: "HEH HEH... THAT LAMP IS COOL." Butt-Head: "UH... FLIP IT, BEAVIS." Nachos on the table. TV shows flip-ly.net. A lobster sits between them judging. "THIS SUCKS... NO WAIT, THIS IS COOL."

Pixel art of the "Nyan Cat" but it's a "Nyan Lobster" flying through space leaving a rainbow trail of garage sale items — lamps, records, ceramic cats, vintage Pyrex. Pop-tart body replaced with a "DEAL SCORE: NYAN/10" badge. 8-bit music notes float. Stars are tiny price tags. Score counter: 999,999 DEALS.

Pixel art of the "Roller Coaster Tycoon" game screen but the park is called "FLIP-LY LAND." Rides include "THE LAMP DROP," "CERAMIC CAT COASTER," and "VINYL SPIN." Park guests carry garage sale bags. A status popup: "Guest 847 says 'I found a deal!'" A lobster mascot waves at the entrance. Park rating: EXCELLENT.

Pixel art of "Who Wants to Be a Millionaire" set. Regis asks: "FOR $1,000,000 — WHAT IS THE DEAL SCORE OF A $3 VINTAGE KITCHENAID?" Lifelines used: 50/50, Phone-a-Lobster, Ask the Garage Sale. The contestant sweats. Audience holds up signs. Answer choices: A) 6/10 B) 8/10 C) 10/10 D) PRICELESS.

Pixel art of the "All Your Base Are Belong To Us" meme. Zero Wing intro screen but the text reads "ALL YOUR DEALS ARE BELONG TO US." "SOMEBODY SET UP US THE GARAGE SALE." "YOU HAVE NO CHANCE TO SURVIVE MAKE YOUR FLIP." Captain is a lobster. Bridge crew panics. "MAIN SCREEN TURN ON" shows flip-ly.net.

Pixel art of Crocodile Hunter Steve Irwin but he's stalking a vintage lamp in its natural habitat — a suburban driveway at dawn. "CRIKEY! LOOK AT THE SIZE OF THAT DEAL!" He carefully approaches. The lamp doesn't notice. Camera crew follows. A lobster assistant holds a net. "SHE'S A BEAUTY — DEAL SCORE: 10/10!"

Pixel art of the "Disaster Girl" meme. The little girl smiles menacingly in the foreground while a garage sale burns (metaphorically — prices slashed, chaos everywhere, shoppers fighting over finds). She holds a vintage lamp. Behind her: "EVERYTHING 50% OFF FINAL HOUR." A lobster fire truck arrives too late.

Pixel art of the "Hide the Pain Harold" meme but Harold is at a garage sale. He smiles through the pain of seeing someone else grab the vintage KitchenAid he'd been eyeing. Eyes dead inside. Smile forced. Background: sunny suburban driveway. A lobster pats his shoulder sympathetically. Deal score: PAIN/10.

Pixel art of Space Jam. Michael Jordan dunks but the basketball is a vintage lamp. The Monstars are overpriced retail stores. Bugs Bunny wears a flip-ly.net jersey. The court floor is a giant Craigslist screenshot. Scoreboard: TUNE SQUAD (DEALS) 99 — MONSTARS (RETAIL) 12. A lobster is the referee. Charles Barkley watches from the stands.

Pixel art of the "You vs The Guy She Told You Not to Worry About" meme. Left side: a sad new IKEA lamp, $49.99, basic. Right side: a magnificent vintage Tiffany-style lamp, $3 at a garage sale, glowing with confidence. The vintage lamp wears tiny sunglasses. A lobster awards the vintage lamp a rose. Deal score floats overhead.

Pixel art of the Oregon Trail game but modern. "You have died of DEAL FEVER." The wagon is a minivan overflowing with vintage finds. The trail map shows a route through suburban garage sales. Party members: LobsterKing (healthy), ButlerBot (exhausted), CeramicCat (broken). River crossing options: "CAULK THE MINIVAN AND FLOAT."

Hyper-realistic oil painting style of a single lobster claw gently holding a cracked porcelain teacup in a dark room. One shaft of golden light from a window illuminates dust particles. The teacup has a barely visible "$0.25" sticker. Moody, museum-quality, no text. The kind of image that makes people stop scrolling and ask "what IS this?"

Watercolor splash illustration — loose, dreamy, bleeding colors — of a suburban street at sunrise. Garage sale signs dot every lawn but they look like prayer flags. A woman carries a lamp like she's carrying a newborn. No lobster. No branding. Just vibes. Caption bait: "Saturday mornings hit different when you know."

Minimalist flat vector design. Solid coral background. Center: a single white silhouette of a lamp. Below it in clean sans-serif: "someone's trash. your treasure. flip-ly.net" That's it. No pixel art. No chaos. Just the hook.

Photorealistic AI render of a pristine mid-century modern living room. Every piece of furniture has a tiny garage sale sticker still on it — $3, $7, $12. The room looks like it costs $40,000. Small text overlay in the corner: "Total cost: $47. All from this weekend." No lobster. No pixel art. Pure aspirational flex.

Chaotic Gen-Z meme collage — deep-fried, oversaturated, with impact font and random arrows pointing at nothing. A lobster with laser eyes. A lamp with a crown. Text reads "POV: YOU JUST FOUND A KITCHENAID FOR $5 AND YOUR HANDS ARE SHAKING." Watermark says "flip-ly.net" in the most unhinged font possible.

Noir-style black and white illustration. A detective in a trench coat stands in a rain-soaked alley holding up a lamp to the light. Narration text box: "She walked into my office with a lamp and a story. Said she got it for three bucks at a garage sale downtown. I didn't believe her. Nobody gets a deal that clean." Bottom: flip-ly.net

Studio Ghibli-inspired illustration of a quiet Japanese countryside flea market at golden hour. A small girl carries an oversized ceramic cat almost as big as her. An old vendor smiles. Lanterns glow. Cherry blossoms fall. No text needed — the warmth IS the brand. For the "this is beautiful" share crowd.

Bold typographic post — black background, white text, centered: "I spent $23 at garage sales this weekend. I made $410 on eBay by Tuesday. The math isn't mathing for retail anymore." Below in small red text: "flip-ly.net — where smart flippers start." No images. Just the numbers. Engagement driver for the "save this" crowd.

Vaporwave aesthetic — pink/purple/teal gradients, Greek statue bust wearing VR goggles, floating geometric shapes, and a vintage lamp hovering in a neon grid void. Japanese text that translates to "THE DEALS ARE ETERNAL." A tiny "flip-ly.net" glitches in the corner. Windows 95 taskbar at the bottom. For the aesthetic accounts that reshare anything vaporwave.

3D claymation render — Wallace and Gromit style — of a tiny clay lobster proudly placing a miniature lamp on a dollhouse shelf. The workshop table is covered in clay garage sale finds. Warm stop-motion lighting. Fingerprint textures visible in the clay. Cozy, tactile, the kind of post people send to friends saying "look at this little guy."
